advantages: Immutability prevents accidental mutation when passed around.
Hashable (when elements are) — works as dict keys, set elements and cache keys.
Lighter than a list of the same length.
Clear signal of intent: 'this is a fixed-size record'.
disadvantages: Cannot grow or shrink — wrong choice for collections that evolve.
Positional access is opaque; readers must remember what each slot means (use namedtuple / dataclass for names).
If an element is itself mutable (e.g. a tuple of lists), the tuple becomes unhashable.
The trailing-comma syntax for singletons trips up beginners.
